originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo a global health emergency
title: A Big Doctor Alarm in Our Africa!
content:
Welcome, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hello, my wonderful friends all across our big continent of Africa! I am your friendly news reporter, here to share important news with you today. Sometimes, news can sound a bit scary, but remember, we talk about it so we can understand and stay safe. Today, we have news from a country called DR Congo, and it's about a sickness that grown-ups are working very hard to stop. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the whole wide world! It has many beautiful countries and amazing people, just like you!

What's Happening in DR Congo?

Right now, in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, some people are getting sick. They have a sickness called Ebola. It's a serious sickness, but lots of clever doctors and helpers are working hard to make people better and stop the sickness from spreading. Did you know? The Democratic Republic of Congo is a very big country in Africa, with amazing rivers and rainforests where many special animals live!

What is Ebola, Exactly?

Ebola is caused by a very tiny germ, so small you cannot see it without a special microscope! These tiny germs can make people feel very, very sick. It's not like a common cold or a cough you might get. When someone has Ebola, they can get a very high fever, a bad tummy ache, and feel very, very tired and weak. Did you know? Germs are tiny living things that can sometimes make us sick, but many germs are also good for us!

How Do People Get Sick with Ebola?

It's important to know that Ebola is not like a cold that spreads through the air when someone sneezes. You can't catch it just by being near someone. Instead, people can get Ebola if they touch the body liquids of someone who is very sick with Ebola. These liquids can be things like sweat, blood, or vomit. This is why doctors and nurses who help sick people wear special protective clothes, like superhero suits, to keep themselves safe! Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water for at least 20 seconds is one of the best ways to get rid of germs and keep yourself healthy!

Who is the WHO?

There is a very important group of doctors and health experts called the WHO. This stands for the World Health Organization. Think of them like the world's biggest team of doctors and nurses who help countries all over the globe stay healthy and safe. They give advice and send help when people are sick. Did you know? The WHO works with governments and communities to make sure everyone has a chance to be healthy, no matter where they live.

A "Global Health Emergency" - What's That?

The WHO has said that this Ebola sickness in DR Congo is a "Global Health Emergency." This sounds like a very big and serious name, right? It means it's like a really big alarm bell! It's a way for the WHO to tell the whole world: "Hey, we need to send lots of help, doctors, medicine, and support to DR Congo right now to stop this sickness!" Did you know? An "emergency" means something important needs attention and help very, very quickly!

Why is it an Emergency?

It's an emergency because many people have gotten sick in DR Congo. Around 246 people have fallen ill with Ebola. And sadly, around 80 people have died because of it. That's why the doctors and helpers need to act fast. They want to make sure no more people get sick and that those who are sick can get better. It's a serious situation for the people living there. Did you know? Many kind and brave people, like doctors and nurses, travel to places like DR Congo to help others when they need it most.

But It's Not Everywhere!

It's important to remember that even though it's a "Global Health Emergency," it does *not* mean that everyone in Africa or around the world is going to get sick with Ebola. It means that the world is focusing its help on the places in DR Congo where people are getting sick. It's about containing the sickness, which means keeping it in one place and stopping it from spreading far and wide. Did you know? Africa has 54 different countries, each with its own special cultures and languages!

How Can We Stay Safe and Healthy?

Even though Ebola is far away for most of us, there are always important things we can do to stay healthy and strong every day! These are very good habits: 1. **Wash your hands!** Use soap and clean water often, especially before eating and after using the toilet. Wash them for as long as it takes to sing "Happy Birthday" twice! 2. **Don't touch your face!** Try not to touch your eyes, nose, or mouth with your hands, especially if your hands are not clean. 3. **Tell a grown-up!** If you ever feel sick, have a fever, or a tummy ache, tell your parents, teachers, or any trusted adult right away. 4. **Listen to grown-ups!** Always listen to your parents and teachers because they know how to keep you safe and healthy. Did you know? Good hygiene, like hand washing, can help stop many kinds of germs from making us sick!

Our Heroes in Action!

There is good news! Many smart and brave people are working as fast as they can in DR Congo. Doctors are giving medicine, nurses are taking care of sick people, and scientists are trying to find even better ways to fight the Ebola germ. They are also teaching people in DR Congo how to stay safe and healthy, and how to stop the sickness from spreading. Did you know? Scientists are like detectives who study how germs work and find ways to create medicines to make us feel better!

Let's Keep Our Africa Healthy and Strong!

So, my dear friends, while this news is serious for the people in DR Congo, we know that many kind people are helping. We can all do our part by staying clean, being kind to each other, and listening to the grown-ups who care for us. Let's all wish our friends in DR Congo strength and health. We are one big, strong African family, and we look out for each other! Did you know? When we are kind and help each other, it makes our world a happier and stronger place for everyone!
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared an Ebola sickness in DR Congo a "Global Health Emergency," meaning many doctors and helpers are sending urgent help to stop the tiny germ from making more people sick and to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it tells everyone that people in DR Congo need our help right now to stop a serious sickness called Ebola, so that fewer people get sick and everyone can stay healthy and safe.

originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Friends Missed in Nigeria: Let's Wish Them Home!
content:
Hello, Friends!

Welcome to our special news report for young learners across Africa! Today, we have some important news from a place called Nigeria, which is a very big country right here in Africa. Sometimes, sad things happen, but it's important to talk about them and also share hope.

Sad News from Mussa Town

In a small town called Mussa, in a part of Nigeria called Borno state, something very sad happened. Many children were at school, learning and playing, just like you do. But then, some people came and took them away from their school. This made their families and friends very, very sad and worried. We call it 'kidnapped' when someone is taken away without permission. These children were taken from their school in Mussa town. Some of these children were very, very little, like toddlers, who are just starting to learn about the world. Imagine your little brother or sister being taken from school – it's a very sad thought.

Where is Mussa Town?

Mussa town is in Borno state, which is a big area in Nigeria. Nigeria is a huge country in West Africa, known for its vibrant culture, amazing music, and friendly people! It has many different languages and delicious foods. It’s a very important country on our continent. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! This means lots and lots of people live there, all helping to make it a wonderful place.

Why is This News Sad?

This news makes many people sad because school is a safe place. It’s a place where children go to learn new things, make friends, and dream about their future. When children are taken from school, it makes everyone feel worried about their safety and happiness. Parents are missing their children terribly. They want them back home, safe and sound. Teachers are also very sad because they love their students and want them to be in class, learning and laughing. The whole community in Mussa town is hoping and praying for the children to come back soon.

What Are Grown-Ups Doing to Help?

When something like this happens, many grown-ups get to work to help. The police and soldiers in Nigeria are looking everywhere for the children. They are trying their best to find them and bring them back home safely to their families. The government, which is like the big group of grown-ups who help run the country, is also working hard. They are trying to make sure that children can go to school without being scared. They want all schools to be safe places for learning and playing. Did you know? Many schools in Nigeria have bright, colourful uniforms that children wear to show they belong to their school! They look very smart.

Why is School So Important?

School is a super important place for every child. It's where you learn to read stories, write your name, count numbers, and discover amazing things about the world. School helps you grow smart and strong, so you can have a wonderful future. At school, you also get to play with your friends, share your lunch, and learn about being kind to each other. It’s a place where friendships grow, and big dreams begin. That's why it's so important for all children to be able to go to school safely.

We All Want Our Friends to Be Safe

Every child, everywhere in Africa and the world, deserves to feel safe. They deserve to go to school, play outside, and sleep soundly in their beds without any worries. The grown-ups who care for them are trying very hard to make sure this happens. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with many different countries, and in almost all of them, children love to play football (what some call soccer)! It’s a favourite game for many.

A Message of Hope

Even though this news is sad, it’s important to remember that many people are working together. People are praying for the children in Mussa town to come back home. People all over Nigeria and other parts of Africa are thinking about them and their families. We must always have hope. Hope is a strong feeling that tells us good things can happen, even after sad times. Let's all send our kind thoughts and wishes to the children and their families in Mussa town, hoping they will be reunited very, very soon. Did you know? The baobab tree, a famous tree in Africa, can live for thousands of years and is sometimes called the 'tree of life' because it provides food, water, and shelter for many creatures! Let’s remember to be kind to each other and appreciate our safe schools and homes. And let’s keep hoping for the safe return of all the children from Mussa town. Thank you for listening, and remember to always be brave and kind!
summary: Many children, including some very little ones, were sadly taken from their school in Mussa town, Nigeria, making their families and community very worried, but grown-ups are working hard to bring them home safely.
whyMatters: It's important to know this because all children deserve to be safe at school and at home, and we should hope for their quick return and for peace in their community.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Making Africa Safer for Kids!
content:
Hello, Super Star Kids of Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something important. We have some big news from our amazing continent, Africa. It's news about making our homes and communities safer for everyone, especially for all of you wonderful children. Sometimes, grown-ups work very hard to stop bad things from happening. This story is about one of those times. It shows how people can work together to make the world a better place. Are you ready to hear about it? Let's go!

A Very Bad Leader Stopped

There was a person named Abu-Bilal al-Minuki. He was a very bad leader. He led a group that caused a lot of trouble and made many people sad and scared. This group hurt people and tried to make places unsafe. Imagine someone who doesn't want people to live in peace. That's what he was like. He was known for causing a lot of problems in different parts of the world, especially where our friends live in Africa. It's like a bully who makes everyone unhappy. We don't like bullies, do we? Did you know? Even grown-ups sometimes have to deal with bullies, just like you might at school. But these bullies are much bigger and cause bigger problems!

What is a "Joint Operation"?

Now, how was this bad leader stopped? It happened because of something called a "joint operation." Imagine you and your friend want to build a super cool sandcastle. You bring the buckets, and your friend brings the shovels. You work together to make the best sandcastle ever! That's a joint operation! It means different groups or countries worked together as a team. In this case, two important countries decided to team up. They shared their helpers and their plans. They wanted to stop Abu-Bilal al-Minuki from hurting more people. Working together made them strong. Did you know? When countries work together, it's called cooperation! Cooperation helps solve big problems, just like when you cooperate with your friends to finish a game or a project.

Who Helped Make Us Safer?

So, which countries were our heroes in this story? It was Nigeria and the United States of America. Nigeria is a big and beautiful country right here in Africa. The United States is a country far away, across the big ocean. Both countries decided that it was important to stop this bad leader. They sent their brave helpers to work together. They made a plan and carried it out. It was a very big and important job. Our brave helpers worked hard to make sure that the bad leader could not cause any more harm. They wanted to protect families and children. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! It has so many amazing people and cultures.

Why is This Good News?

Stopping Abu-Bilal al-Minuki is very good news for everyone. When a bad leader who causes trouble is stopped, it means places can start to become safer. Children can play more freely. Families can feel more secure. It means that there is more hope for peace and quiet in places where there was trouble before. Imagine a very noisy and messy room, and then someone helps clean it up and make it tidy and peaceful again. That's a bit like what happened. This helps bring a bit more sunshine back into people's lives. It shows that good people can win against bad people. Did you know? Peace means a time when there is no fighting or trouble. Everyone wants to live in peace!

What Does "President Trump Said"?

You might hear grown-ups talk about "President Trump." He was the leader of the United States. He said that this operation was very important. He called Abu-Bilal al-Minuki "the most active terrorist in the world." That's a big grown-up word. For us, it means he was someone who caused a lot of trouble and hurt in many places. President Trump was happy that this bad leader was stopped. It means a big problem was taken care of. When important leaders say things like this, it shows how big and important the news is. It makes people around the world feel a little bit safer. Did you know? A president is like the head teacher of a whole country! They help make big decisions for everyone.

More About Keeping Safe

This story reminds us that there are many brave people who work hard every day to keep us safe. They are like our superheroes, but real ones! They might be police officers, soldiers, or other special helpers. They train hard and work together to stop bad things from happening. It's a very serious job, but it helps all of us. When they stop bad leaders, it means less trouble for everyone. We should be thankful for their hard work. It helps us sleep better at night, knowing good people are watching out for us. Did you know? There are many different kinds of jobs that help keep people safe, like firefighters, doctors, and even people who design safe roads!

What This Means for Children

For you, our wonderful children of Africa, this means a little bit more peace. It means that efforts are being made to build a brighter future where you can grow up safe and strong. When bad leaders are stopped, it creates more chances for schools to stay open, for families to be together, and for communities to thrive. This news is a step towards a world where all children can play, learn, and dream without fear. It's about building a better Africa for tomorrow. Keep being kind, keep learning, and remember that good people are always working for a better world! Did you know? Playing and learning are super important for children to grow up happy and healthy!

Our African Home

Africa is a huge continent, full of amazing animals, beautiful landscapes, and incredible people. Like any big family, sometimes there are problems. But stories like this show that we, as Africans, are strong. And we have friends around the world who want to help us. Together, we can make our continent an even more wonderful place to live, full of peace and happiness. Let's all do our part, big or small, to be kind and helpful. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries!

Wrapping Up Our News

So, to wrap up our big news: a very bad leader named Abu-Bilal al-Minuki was stopped. This happened because Nigeria and the United States worked together like a great team. This is good news because it helps make our world, especially parts of Africa, a safer and more peaceful place for everyone. It shows that when good people work together, they can stop bad things. Let's keep hoping for a world full of peace, kindness, and safety for all children. That's all for today, super star kids! Stay safe and keep shining!
summary: A very bad leader who caused trouble was stopped by brave helpers from Nigeria and the United States, making places safer for children in Africa.
whyMatters: This is important because it means fewer bad people causing trouble, which helps make our homes and communities in Africa more peaceful and safe for all children to live and play.

originalTitle: Republican Senator Cassidy loses Louisiana primary after opposing Trump
title: Senator Bill Cassidy's Big Decision!
content:
Hello, my friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you stories from all around the big, wide world! Today, we're going on an adventure to a far-away country called America. It's a land with many states, just like we have many countries in Africa!

A Special Leader Called a Senator

In America, there are special people called "senators." Think of a senator like a big brother or sister who helps make rules for a whole family. They work in a very important building and talk about what's best for everyone. Senator Bill Cassidy is one of these special leaders. He works for a place in America called Louisiana. Louisiana is a state, like a big province, with lots of people and fun things to do! Did you know? The word "senator" comes from a very old word that means "old man" or "elder"! It's because long ago, only older, wise people were chosen for these important jobs.

Teams of Leaders: Republicans

In America, leaders often belong to different "teams." Just like when you play soccer or netball, you have different teams, right? In politics, these teams have names. One big team is called the "Republicans." Senator Bill Cassidy was on the Republican team. He worked with other Republicans to try and make America a better place, just like different teams in school might work together on projects. Sometimes, people on the same team don't always agree on everything. That's okay! It happens in school too, doesn't it? Even friends can have different ideas and still be friends.

A Big Day in America: January 6, 2021

Now, let's talk about something important that happened a little while ago. On a day called January 6, in the year 2021, something very noisy happened in America. Some people were very, very angry. They were angry about an election. An election is when people choose their leaders by voting. When people are angry, they sometimes do things that are not very helpful or kind. These angry people went to a super important building called the Capitol. This building is where the senators and other leaders work to make rules for the country. It's like the biggest, most important meeting house in the whole country! Did you know? The Capitol Building in America has a giant dome on top! It's like a big, round hat that you can see from far away!

Senator Cassidy Made a Big Choice

After this noisy day at the Capitol, the leaders needed to talk about what happened. They needed to decide if another very important leader, named Donald Trump, had done anything wrong. Donald Trump was the President of America at that time. Senator Bill Cassidy had to make a very big choice. He listened to all the talk, thought very hard, and then he made his decision. He decided that he believed Donald Trump had done something wrong, based on what happened that day. When a senator makes such a choice, it's called "voting to convict." It means they think someone should be held responsible for their actions. It's like when you have to decide if someone broke a rule in a game and needs to say sorry.

Why His Choice Was Important

Most of the other leaders on his Republican team did not agree with Senator Cassidy. They thought Donald Trump had not done anything wrong. So, Senator Cassidy stood up and said what he believed, even though it was different from many of his teammates. It takes a lot of courage to stand up for what you believe is right, especially when your friends or team members might not agree with you. It's like choosing to tell the truth even if it means you might get into a little trouble yourself. Did you know? In many countries in Africa, we also have parliaments and leaders who make important decisions for everyone. They also have different teams or groups that try to make the country better for all its people!

What is a "Primary" Election?

Now, let's talk about something called a "primary election." It sounds a bit like "prime," which means number one! But it's a little different. Imagine you have a big race to choose the best runner for your school. Before the big race, you might have a smaller race just for your class, to see who the fastest person in your class is. That smaller race is like a "primary election." A primary election is when people from the same team, like the Republicans, choose who they want to represent their team in the *next* big election. It's like a try-out before the main event where they pick their star player!

Senator Cassidy Did Not Win

So, Senator Bill Cassidy wanted to keep being a senator for Louisiana. He wanted to run in the next big election. But first, he needed to win the "primary election" for his Republican team. Because he made that big choice to vote against Donald Trump, many people on his Republican team in Louisiana were not happy with him. They did not like his decision to go against their former President. When it was time for the primary election, many people in his team chose someone else instead of him. This means Senator Cassidy "lost" the primary. He did not get enough votes from his team to go on to the next big election and try to keep his job. Did you know? In some places, people use special machines to vote, and in other places, they use paper ballots and put them in a box! It's like choosing your favorite snack by putting a pebble in a jar!

What We Learn From This Story

This story teaches us that making choices can be hard, especially for leaders. Sometimes, when you make a choice that you think is right, not everyone will agree with you. And sometimes, those disagreements can have big results, like losing an election. Senator Cassidy made a choice that he believed was good and fair for his country. But because many people on his team disagreed, he didn't get to keep his job as a senator for now. It shows us that in a big country, people have many different ideas. It's important to listen to everyone, even if you don't always agree. And it's important for leaders to be brave and make decisions they truly believe in for their people. Every vote counts, whether it's in a primary election or a big election. It's how people show what they want and who they want to lead them. It's their voice! So, even though this happened in a far-away place, it reminds us that leaders everywhere have important jobs and make big choices every day. And people get to decide who they want to be their leader. That's how democracy works! That's all for today's news from our big world! Stay curious, my friends!
summary: Senator Bill Cassidy from America lost his chance to be a leader again because he made a brave choice to disagree with another important leader, Donald Trump, which many people on his team did not like.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows us that leaders have to make hard choices, and sometimes those choices mean people will agree or disagree with them, which can change who gets to be a leader in a country.

originalTitle: Javier Bardem says condemning the Gaza genocide has led to more work
title: A Brave Actor's Kind Heart!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi there, amazing young adventurers! It's your friendly reporter here from Africa! We have a really cool story today about a famous grown-up who used his voice to do something wonderful. It's a story about being kind, being brave, and how doing good can make even more good come your way! Are you ready? Let's go!

Who is Javier Bardem?

Our story is about a grown-up named Javier Bardem. Javier is a very famous actor! Do you know what an actor does? An actor is like a super storyteller! They pretend to be different people in movies and TV shows. They can make you laugh, they can make you excited, and sometimes, they can even make you feel a little bit sad. They use their voices and their bodies to tell amazing stories that we all love to watch. Did you know? Actors sometimes spend a lot of time learning new things for their roles, like how to ride a horse or speak a different language! It's like doing homework for grown-ups, but super fun!

When Friends Need Help

Sometimes, in our big, big world, there are places where people are having a very hard time. Maybe they don't have enough food, or they are sad, or things just aren't fair for them. When grown-ups see this, grown-ups who have kind hearts want to help. It's like when you see your friend trip and fall down – what do you do? You run to help them up, right? Or if someone is being unkind to a classmate, what do you do? You might tell a teacher, or you might say, "Hey, that's not fair!" That's called speaking up.

Javier's Big Voice

Well, Javier, our famous actor, saw some people in the world who were really, really sad and needed help. He saw that things were not fair for them. So, what did he do? He used his big, famous voice! He spoke out and said, "We need to be kind to these people! We need to help them!" He told everyone that these people were in trouble and deserved kindness and care. This was a very brave thing to do, because sometimes, speaking up can be a little bit scary. Did you know? Being brave doesn't mean you're not scared at all. It means you feel a little scared, but you still do what is right and important! You are brave every time you try something new or stand up for a friend.

A Wonderful Surprise!

Now, sometimes, when people speak up about important things, others might not like it. Some might think, "Oh no, maybe this famous actor will get into trouble if he talks about sad things." Or, "Maybe he won't get any more movie jobs if he keeps speaking up." But guess what? Something truly wonderful happened!

More Movies for Javier!

Because Javier was so kind and so brave, people actually liked him even more! Movie directors and people who make movies saw his kind heart. They saw that he was not just a good actor, but also a good person who cared about others. They thought, "Wow, this person is so strong and caring! We want him in our movies!" So, instead of getting fewer jobs, Javier actually got *more* jobs! He got more chances to act and tell stories because he chose to use his voice for good. It's like when you are a really good friend at school, and you share your toys and help others. More children want to play with you, right? It's the same idea! When you are kind and brave, good things can happen.

What We Learn from Javier

This story teaches us a very important lesson, my friends. It teaches us that being kind is always a superpower. Using your voice to help people who are sad or who need things to be fair is one of the best things you can do. It shows everyone that you have a good heart. And sometimes, like for Javier, doing the right thing can bring even more good things into your life. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, we have a special word called "Ubuntu." It means "I am because we are." It teaches us that we are all connected, like a big family. When you help someone else, you are helping yourself and everyone around you!

Your Turn to Be Brave and Kind!

You, my amazing young friends, also have a voice! You have a kind heart! You can be brave every day. * You can speak up for your friend if someone is being mean. * You can share your favourite snack with someone who doesn't have one. * You can help your parents with chores around the house. * You can be kind to animals and plants. Every little kind thing you do, every time you use your voice for good, you make our world a better, brighter place. Just like Javier, you can be a superhero of kindness and bravery!

Keep Shining Bright!

So remember the story of Javier, the kind actor who used his voice for good and got even more chances to shine. Be brave, be kind, and always, always use your voice to make the world a happier place for everyone. Until next time, keep being the wonderful, amazing kids you are! Bye for now!
summary: A famous actor named Javier Bardem spoke up to help people who were sad, and because he was so kind and brave, he actually got more movie jobs!
whyMatters: This is important because it shows us that being kind, brave, and speaking up for what is right can bring good things into your life and make the world a better place for everyone.

originalTitle: The strike that changed the geometry of war
title: Amazing New Ways to Do Things, Even Far Away!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something interesting that happened far, far away. Sometimes, grown-ups find clever new ways to do things. It's like when you learn a new trick with your skipping rope or when your mum finds a new recipe that makes dinner extra yummy! These new ways can sometimes be a bit surprising, like a magic trick you didn't expect. Today, we are talking about how people have found a new way to reach places that are very, very far away. Imagine you want to send a message to your cousin who lives in another town. You might write a letter, or maybe even call them on a phone. But what if you could do something even more amazing and unexpected from a super long distance? That's what some people have found a way to do!

What are these New Ways?

Think about throwing a ball. You can throw it to your friend next to you, right? But what if you could throw it so far that it lands across a big field, or even in another village? That's kind of what happened! People have invented new tools and new smart ways to make things happen, even if they are very, very far from where they want something to go. It's like when we learned to fly in aeroplanes! Long, long ago, people could only walk or ride animals. Then someone invented the bicycle, then the car, and then, wow, the aeroplane! Each new invention changed how we travelled and how we reached places far away. These new ways of doing things are like those big changes, but for sending things or making things happen from a distance. Did you know? The fastest land animal in Africa is the cheetah! It can run so fast, like a blur, to catch its food. But even a cheetah can't run from one end of a country to another in a few minutes!

Reaching Far, Far Away!

Imagine you are building a tall tower with blocks. You usually put the blocks on top of each other with your hands. But what if you had a super-long arm, like a robot arm, that could put the blocks on top of your tower even if you were standing far away? That's a little bit like this new discovery. People have found ways to do things over a very big distance, like from one big city to another big city that is many, many miles away. It’s not magic, it’s cleverness! Scientists and smart people are always thinking of new ways to use technology. Technology is all the cool stuff like phones, computers, and even special kinds of rockets or flying machines. They use these things to make what seemed impossible, possible. Did you know? Messages used to be carried by people walking or riding horses for many, many days or weeks! Now, with phones and the internet, we can send a message to almost anywhere in the world in just a few seconds! Isn't that amazing?

Why is it a Bit Surprising?

Sometimes, when people find new ways to do things from far away, it can be a little bit surprising. Why? Because it changes how we usually think things work. It's like if you always played a game with certain rules, and then one day, someone introduces a new rule that makes the game a bit different and harder to guess what will happen next. That's what 'unpredictable' means – a bit hard to guess. For example, if you are playing hide-and-seek, and your friend suddenly finds a new, super clever hiding spot that no one has ever used before, it's surprising! You didn't expect it. These new ways of reaching far away are like that super clever hiding spot. They make things a bit harder to guess for everyone. Did you know? The Earth is always spinning! But we don't feel it because it's always moving smoothly. If it suddenly wobbled, that would be very surprising and unpredictable!

Learning About New Things

It's important for us to learn about these new things that grown-ups discover. Even though these new ways might be big and complicated for now, understanding that people are always inventing and changing things is good. It helps us know that the world is always moving forward and finding new ways to do things. Just like you learn new things at school every day – new letters, new numbers, new stories – grown-ups are also always learning and inventing new things. Sometimes these inventions are for good things, like new medicines or better ways to grow food. Sometimes, they are things that help people reach far away. Did you know? Your brain is always learning new things, even when you are sleeping! It's like a super computer that never stops trying to understand the world around you.

What Does This Mean for Us?

For us, here in Africa, and for children everywhere, this news means that the world is always changing. It means that new ideas and new inventions are happening all the time. Sometimes these inventions are exciting, and sometimes they make us think about how we can all live together peacefully. It's important to remember that even when grown-ups find clever new ways to do things, the most important thing is for everyone to be kind, to share, and to help each other. We all live on this one big Earth, and being good friends and neighbours is the best way to make sure our world is a happy and safe place for everyone. Did you know? Many amazing inventions have come from Africa, like early forms of mathematics and even some of the first surgical tools! We are full of clever minds! So, my little friends, let's keep learning, keep playing, and always remember to be kind to each other. That's the best news of all! See you next time!
summary: People have found surprising new ways to do things and reach places that are very, very far away, which changes how things normally work and can be a bit hard to guess.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that new inventions are always changing our world, making it possible to do things from a distance that we couldn't before.

originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo a global health emergency
title: Big News: Helping Our Friends in DR Congo Stay Healthy!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

It's your kid-friendly news reporter, coming to you from our wonderful continent of Africa! I have some important news to share today. We are going to talk about a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a very big country, just like our home, and it has beautiful jungles and lots of amazing animals!

What is Ebola?

Imagine something so tiny, tiny, tiny that you cannot see it with your eyes. It's much smaller than a speck of dust! These super tiny things are called germs, and some of them are called viruses. Sometimes, these tiny viruses can make people feel very sick. Ebola is one of these viruses.

How Does Ebola Make People Sick?

When someone gets the Ebola virus, they can start to feel very unwell. They might get a high fever, which means their body feels very hot. They might have a stomach ache, feel very tired, and get headaches. It can be like a very bad flu, but much, much more serious. Our bodies are very clever and usually fight off germs, but Ebola is a tricky one.

What is an 'Outbreak'?

When many people in one place suddenly start getting sick from the same germ, we call it an "outbreak." It means the sickness is spreading in that area. It's like when many kids in your class get the same cough or cold, but with Ebola, it's a bigger, more serious problem.

News from DR Congo

Sadly, in DR Congo, some people have gotten sick with the Ebola virus. We have heard that about 246 people have gotten sick. And, about 80 friends have sadly passed away because of this sickness. This makes us feel very sad for our brothers and sisters in DR Congo. But the good news is, many kind and smart people are working very hard to help them.

Who is the WHO?

There is a very special group of doctors, nurses, and health helpers from all over the world. They are called the World Health Organization, or WHO for short. You can think of them like super doctors who help countries all around the globe stay healthy and safe from sickness. They give advice and send help.

What is a 'Global Health Emergency'?

The WHO recently said that the Ebola problem in DR Congo is a "global health emergency." Wow, that sounds like a very big and important phrase, doesn't it? It means that this sickness is a serious problem, and it needs everyone's attention and help from all over the world. It's like a big, loud alarm bell ringing, telling us, "Hey, we need to help our friends in DR Congo right now!"

How Does Ebola Spread?

This is important to know! Ebola is not like a cold or flu that can easily spread through the air when someone coughs or sneezes. Instead, it spreads when you touch someone who is very, very sick, or when you touch their blood, sweat, or other body fluids. That's why doctors and nurses wear special suits, gloves, and masks to protect themselves.

How Can We Stay Safe from Germs?

The best way to stay safe from many, many germs, including ones that can make you sick, is to wash your hands! Use soap and water and wash your hands for a long time. You can sing "Happy Birthday" twice in your head while you wash to make sure you wash long enough! Always wash your hands before eating and after going to the toilet. Try not to touch your face with dirty hands.

Did You Know?

Washing your hands properly can get rid of millions of tiny germs! It's like having a superpower that keeps you healthy and strong! So, keep those hands super clean!

Heroes Are Helping!

Many brave doctors, nurses, and kind helpers are working every single day in DR Congo. They are giving medicine to sick people, taking care of them, and teaching everyone how to stay safe and healthy. They are true heroes, working very hard to make sure the sickness stops spreading and everyone gets better.

What Else Is Being Done?

Scientists from all over the world are also working very hard. They are trying to find special medicines that can fight Ebola, and also vaccines. Vaccines are like tiny shields that we can put into our bodies to help them fight off germs before we even get sick. We hope they find these soon to help everyone!

Did You Know?

Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, each with its own special stories, beautiful places, and amazing people. DR Congo is just one of these many wonderful countries.

Why This Is Important for Everyone

Even though DR Congo might be far away from where you live, we are all part of one big family – the family of Africa, and the family of the world! When one part of our big family needs help, we all try our best to help them. It shows we care about each other.

A Message of Hope

It can feel a little bit scary to hear about sickness, but remember this: there are so many smart and kind people working very, very hard right now to stop Ebola and make everyone healthy again. We can all do our part by learning about health, staying clean, and being kind to others.

Let's Be Kind

If you ever hear about someone being sick, remember to always be kind. Sickness is not anyone's fault, and people need our kindness and understanding. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for health and strength to our friends in DR Congo.

Did You Know?

The Democratic Republic of Congo is home to many unique animals, including the okapi! An okapi looks a bit like a zebra because it has stripes on its legs, but it's related to the giraffe! Isn't that cool?
summary: Doctors and health helpers are working hard to stop Ebola in DR Congo, which is a serious sickness that needs help from all around the world.
whyMatters: This matters because Ebola can make people very sick, and many kind people are helping our friends in DR Congo to get better and stop the sickness from spreading, showing we care for everyone in our big world family.

originalTitle: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
title: News from Far Away: What Happened in a City Called Moscow
content:
Hello, Wonderful Kids!

Hello, my amazing friends! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa, and today we're going to talk about some news from very far away. It's important to learn about what happens all over our big, beautiful world. Today, we're looking at news from a place called Europe, which is another continent, far across the sea from us. Sometimes, countries that are far away have big disagreements, just like sometimes friends might have a small disagreement. We always hope everyone can be kind and friendly.

News from Far Away

Imagine a very big city, super far from where we live. This city is called Moscow, and it's the main city in a very, very big country called Russia. Something happened there recently that we want to understand together. Some special flying machines, called drones, flew into Moscow. When they flew there, they hit some buildings. This made some noise, and sadly, some buildings got broken, and a few people got hurt. But don't worry, everyone is working to make things safe again.

What Are Drones?

Have you ever seen a small toy airplane or helicopter that flies in the sky? Or perhaps a camera that flies high up to take pictures? Well, drones are a bit like that! They are flying machines, but no person sits inside them. Someone controls them with a special remote control from the ground, or sometimes they can fly all by themselves using a computer. They can be used for many good things, like delivering packages, helping farmers check their crops, or even taking amazing videos from high up in the sky. But sometimes, sadly, people use them to send messages or cause damage when countries are having disagreements. Did you know? The word 'drone' also means a type of bee! But the flying machines are very different from a buzzing bee.

Where is Moscow? Where is Russia?

Let's imagine our planet Earth. It's round like a giant ball! We live in Africa, which is a big part of that ball. Now, if you travel very far north from Africa, across the Mediterranean Sea, you will reach a continent called Europe. Russia is the biggest country in the world! It's so big that it stretches across two continents – Europe and Asia. Moscow is its capital city, which means it's a very important city, like the heart of the country. It has many tall buildings, famous old buildings, and lots of people. Did you know? Russia is so big that when the sun is rising in one part of Russia, it might still be nighttime in another part!

And Where is Ukraine?

Now, very close to Russia, there is another country called Ukraine. It's also in Europe. Ukraine is a beautiful country with green fields and many lovely cities. For a long time, Russia and Ukraine have been having a very big disagreement. This disagreement has sadly led to fighting. It's like when two friends can't agree on something, but on a much, much bigger scale, involving entire countries. Everyone wishes for them to find peace and friendship again. Did you know? Ukraine is known for its beautiful sunflower fields. Sunflowers are tall, yellow flowers that always turn their heads to face the sun!

What Happened with the Drones?

So, back to our news from Moscow. Recently, some of these drones, the flying machines we talked about, flew into the city of Moscow. They hit some buildings there. The news said that these drones were sent by Ukraine. They flew into Moscow and caused some damage. It was a surprising event for the people living in Moscow. Sadly, when things like this happen, buildings can get broken, and sometimes, people get hurt. Everyone hopes that no one gets hurt badly and that things can be fixed quickly. It reminds us that disagreements can have sad consequences. Did you know? Many big cities have special systems to help stop things like drones from flying where they shouldn't!

Why Did This Happen?

Now, this is an important part to understand. Ukraine said they sent these drones to Moscow because Russia has also been sending its own drones and other things to cities in Ukraine. It's like a cycle of back and forth. Imagine if someone kept knocking on your door, and then you knocked on their door back. It's a sad situation where both sides feel they are responding to what the other side has done. Ukraine's leader said they did it because Russia had been doing similar things to their cities, causing damage and hurting people there. It's a very complicated situation, and many people around the world are hoping that both countries can stop fighting and talk about their problems peacefully. Fighting always brings sadness.

Thinking About Peace

Learning about news from far away, even when it's a bit sad, helps us understand our world better. It teaches us about different countries and how important it is for everyone to live together in peace and kindness. When countries fight, it's never good. People get hurt, homes get broken, and everyone becomes sad. It's much better when people talk to each other, listen, and try to find happy solutions. Did you know? The United Nations is a big group of countries that tries to help countries talk to each other and keep peace in the world!

Let's Hope for Kindness

So, that's the news from Moscow and Ukraine today. We always hope that leaders and people in these countries can find a way to stop fighting. We hope they can choose peace, so that children, families, and everyone can be safe and happy. Remember, no matter where we are in the world, kindness and understanding are always the best way. Let's send our happy thoughts and hopes for peace to everyone, everywhere. See you next time for more news, my friends!
summary: Flying machines called drones went to a city in Russia called Moscow, and Ukraine said they sent them because Russia had also sent similar machines to their cities.
whyMatters: It's important to know about what happens in the world, even far away. When countries disagree, sometimes they fight, and we hope they can find a way to be kind and peaceful instead.

originalTitle: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
title: Brave Helpers in Far-Away Italy!
content:
Jambo kids! That means 'hello' in Swahili! I’m your friendly news reporter, Zawadi, coming to you live from beautiful Africa! I love sharing important stories with you from all over the world. News helps us learn new things and understand what’s happening far away. Sometimes, the news also teaches us super important lessons about staying safe and being kind to one another. Today, we have a story from a place very far from our home, but it’s a story about brave people and why rules are so important for everyone. It’s a story about helping hands!

A Story from Far Away Italy

Our story today takes us to a country called Italy. Can you imagine a country shaped like a boot? That’s Italy! It’s very far from Africa, across the big, blue Mediterranean Sea. Italy is famous for yummy food like pizza and pasta, and beautiful old buildings. Many people live there, just like many people live here in Africa. It’s a place with lots of history and lovely cities. Did you know? Italy is a country in Europe, and its capital city is Rome, where there are amazing ancient buildings like the Colosseum! Knowing about other places is super fun! It’s good to know about different places in the world, even if they are far away. We are all part of one big global family, and we care about each other!

What Happened in Italy?

One sunny day, in a city in Italy, lots of people were walking around. These people are called pedestrians. They were walking on the special path made just for people, which we call a pavement or a sidewalk. The pavement is a safe place for walkers, away from the cars. Cars have their own special place to drive – the road! This is a very important rule for keeping everyone safe. But on this day, something unexpected happened. A car drove onto the pavement where people were walking. This is not where cars are supposed to be at all! Because the car went where it shouldn’t, some of the people walking got bumps and ouchies. Eight people felt unwell and needed some help because of this accident. Did you know? A pedestrian is simply a person who is walking, especially in an area used by vehicles. So, when you walk on the pavement, you are a pedestrian!

Brave Helpers Step In!

But guess what? Even though it was a surprising and a bit scary moment, good people were there! Other people who were nearby saw what was happening. We call these kind people "passers-by" – they were just walking past! These brave passers-by knew something was wrong. They didn't just watch; they decided to act! They bravely ran and helped to stop the car. By stopping the car, they made sure that no more people would get hurt. They were super quick and super brave, like real-life superheroes who don't wear capes! They also knew what to do next. They quickly called for help! When we see someone in trouble, it's always good to tell a grown-up or call for help, like the police or ambulance services. These helpers come very fast. Did you know? Being a "Good Samaritan" means being a kind person who helps someone in need, just like these brave people in Italy! It's a wonderful thing to do.

Getting Better and Feeling Well

The people who got bumps and ouchies needed care. So, doctors and nurses came to help them. Some had bigger ouchies than others, but doctors and nurses are super good at fixing things and making people feel better! They went to a special building called a hospital. A hospital is a wonderful place where doctors, nurses, and other helpers work hard to heal people and make them healthy again. They take care of everyone. We send our best wishes all the way from Africa to Italy, hoping all those who got hurt feel better very, very soon. We want them to be playing and smiling again! Did you know? Doctors and nurses work very hard all over the world to help sick and injured people. They are amazing heroes in their white coats, always ready to lend a helping hand!

Why Rules Keep Us Safe

This story from Italy reminds us of a very important lesson: rules are here to keep us safe! Just like we have rules at school or at home, we have rules for cars and for walking. Cars have their special roads to drive on. These roads are for fast-moving vehicles. People have their special pavements or sidewalks to walk on. This way, everyone stays safe and happy. Imagine if cars drove everywhere! It would be very confusing and dangerous. That’s why we have clear lines and signs to tell cars where to go and where not to go. When you are walking near a road, what do your grown-ups always tell you to do? "Stop, look left, look right, and look left again!" And always hold a grown-up’s hand! This helps you cross safely. Did you know? Traffic lights are like a magic code for cars and people! Red means stop, yellow means get ready, and green means go. They help keep everyone safe at busy crossings.

The Power of Helping Each Other

Even though this was a story about something not so nice, it also shows us something truly wonderful: people help people! When someone is in trouble, kind and brave people step up to help. This spirit of helping is super important. It means we look out for each other, no matter where we are in the world. It makes our communities strong and happy and a better place for everyone. You can be a helper too! Maybe by sharing your toys with a friend, or helping your parents with chores, or telling a grown-up if you see something unsafe. Every little act of kindness makes a big difference. Did you know? Many countries have special numbers to call for emergencies, like 999 or 112. These numbers connect you to police, fire, or ambulance services very quickly!

Staying Safe and Sound

It’s important to remember that most of the time, our roads and paths are safe places. We have good rules, and we have many good people looking out for us, like our parents, teachers, and community helpers. Always listen to your grown-ups about staying safe near roads and cars. They know what’s best for you and want to keep you out of harm’s way and keep you smiling. Let’s all remember to be safe, be kind, and be ready to help if we can. When we work together and follow rules, we make the world a better, safer place for everyone. That’s all for today from your friendly reporter, Zawadi, here in Africa! Keep smiling and keep being amazing! Kwaheri! (Good-bye in Swahili).
summary: In Italy, a car went onto a walking path, causing eight people to get hurt, but brave people nearby quickly helped stop the car and called for more help, showing how important rules and kindness are.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows us that rules, like cars staying on roads and people on pavements, keep us safe. It also teaches us that it's wonderful when people help each other in times of trouble, no matter where they are in the world, and that we should always listen to grown-ups about staying safe.

originalTitle: 'We share same ambition' - Alonso named Chelsea's third boss in year
title: 'We share same ambition' - Alonso named Chelsea's third boss in year
content: Former Liverpool midfielder Xabi Alonso is appointed as Chelsea manager - a day after their FA Cup final defeat by Manchester City.

originalTitle: No trophy in turbulent season but Alonso arrival offers Chelsea hope
title: No trophy in turbulent season but Alonso arrival offers Chelsea hope
content: From the gloom of an FA Cup final loss amid a turbulent campaign, Xabi Alonso's arrival gives Chelsea hope, writes Nizaar Kinsella.

originalTitle: Casemiro's fond farewell - how Brazilian turned Man Utd career around
title: Casemiro's fond farewell - how Brazilian turned Man Utd career around
content: Casemiro plays his last game for Manchester United at Old Trafford against Nottingham Forest, and will return a hero no matter what the future holds after turning his career around at the club.
